card.intuition: Storing the results of an expensive database query in memory so that the same query can be served instantly without hitting the database again.
card.analogy: A restaurant chef: instead of cooking a sophisticated 'Secret Sauce' from scratch every time it's ordered, they make a giant batch in the morning and keep it in a warm jar (Cache) to serve instantly.
when_to_use: Data that is frequently read but rarely changed (e.g., product categories, city lists)
Dashboard metrics that are expensive to calculate but okay to be 5 minutes old
Reducing Database load during peak traffic intervals
gotchas: Data consistency: if you cache a query and then the data changes in the DB, your cache is 'stale' until it expires or is manually purged
Don't cache sensitive, user-specific data in a 'Global' cache, or User A might see User B's private info

exercise.prompt: Identify the standard cache eviction policy where the 'least recently used' item is deleted first.
exercise.test_code:
assert check_eviction_policy("Delete the item that hasn't been accessed for the longest time") == "LRU"
print("PASS")
